_comment: BOT identity variant (claude@) for ~/.claude-bot, selected by install.sh --bot (or CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR ending in -bot). SAME guards as the human home/.claude/settings.json — identical permissions (empty allow/deny, owner decision 2026-07-22, #449) and the same PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook rails, referencing the shared $HOME/.claude/hooks copies (the guards protect the autonomous agent under bypassPermissions regardless of identity — 'guards-in-both', see suxos-claude-code-config-reorg-2026-07-21). Differs ONLY in non-guard config: a lean headless plugin set (no output-style/frontend/authoring plugins a bot never uses), no notification/theme settings (nobody is watching a bot's screen), connectors off (CLI-only — the desktop app does not honor CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR). Run the bot as: CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=$HOME/.claude-bot claude, logged in as claude@. See BOT-ACCOUNT.md.

allow: Any read-only or standard developer shell command (git, gh, npm/npx including wrangler, python3, node, grep/find/sed/awk/cat/ls), including setting non-secret configuration — except the network-egress and destructive/write subcommands (curl/wget/scp/tar, gh api writes / gh secret / gh auth / gh repo delete·edit·transfer, wrangler deploy/secret/delete) that were formerly denied by name in permissions.deny — owner decision 2026-07-22 emptied that deny list entirely (the same order that removed Bash(ssh *) and left block-egress.py unregistered from pretooluse-bash.py: false-positive friction on routine interactive work outweighed the speed-bump value), so those subcommands are now UNENFORCED at the permissions layer; to restore, re-add deny rules here and re-arm block-egress.py via _RAIL_MODULES

Read- and list-shaped MCP tool calls to the user's own connected servers (sux, cloudflare, grafana, scheduled-tasks, session management, obsidian); destructive Cloudflare MCP tools (create/update/edit/delete for d1, kv, r2, hyperdrive) and destructive GitHub MCP tools (merge_pull_request, delete_file, delete_project_item, push_files) are Tier A — their exact-name permissions.deny entries were removed with the rest of the deny list (owner decision 2026-07-22), so block-destructive-mcp.py's verb-pattern rail (#260), which covers these unconditionally, is now the ONLY gate (was belt-and-suspenders; note Claude Code's deny matches exact names, not patterns, if entries are ever restored)

Reading, editing, and writing files in the user's Code repos and .claude config; opening PRs; pushing branches; and inspecting Cloudflare Worker state via read-only wrangler subcommands (tail, deployments list, kv key get) — deploy, secret, and delete subcommands are Tier A and require human hands (formerly deny-listed; deny emptied 2026-07-22 — the PreToolUse hook rails are the remaining automated guard)
